data.body:
G3 child-coverage audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 33d0 "Collapse the dual outbound applier paths to one production path"), 4-bucket per parent criterion, re-cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57). VERDICT: clean pairing — no G3 finding; no uncovered parent criterion remains across the roster.

OWNED — FULLY covered by THIS child:
- Parent AC "Outbound application is collapsed to a single production path (no parallel applier remains)" → 33d0 AC1 (exactly one outbound applier path remains in production). FULLY.
- Parent AC "The retired outbound applier path is deleted" → 33d0 AC1 (retired path deleted; no dead, separately-tested applier code) + 33d0 AC2's behavioral-parity gate before deletion. FULLY.
- Parent SC "Outbound sync is exercised through the production code path, not a parallel dead path" → 33d0 AC1 + AC2 (single multi-sub-op UPDATE — fields+comments+labels+links+parent — driven through the production path, each sub-op asserted). FULLY.
- CONTRIBUTED: parent AC "all six child stories closed; full reconciler suite green" (33d0 AC4 no-regress/suite-green) and parent SC "new tests assert behavior/contract; zero change-detector" (33d0 AC5 asserts effects/sub-op outcomes, never interaction/mock-call-counts).

DELEGATION — parent criteria not owned by this child are correctly assigned to named siblings; the declared OUT item (sub-op telemetry → story E/2359) is consistent with the roster:
- AC SSOT snapshot schema + AC fetcher-validated-vs-schema + AC differ-reads-validated + AC round-trip contract through production path with real fixtures + SC producer/consumers cannot drift → 8937 (AC1/AC2/AC3) + fe57 (fixtures/fake) + 59ef (verified-fake honesty).
- AC sub-op telemetry surfaced + AC computed>0 && applied==0 fails loudly (warn-first) + SC silent no-op fails canary → 2359 (AC1/AC2/AC3).
- AC bidirectional comment/link/parent round-trip asserted hermetically + AC every expected-bug probe guard de-encoded + SC bidirectional round-trips / no probe encodes a bug as expected → 822a.

ANTI-FP / correction to prior audits on this ticket: the recurring "Parent AC6 documented anti-change-detector guardrail only PARTIALLY covered" finding is WITHDRAWN as a false positive. The parent AC reads verbatim "A documented anti-change-detector guardrail for reconciler tests is committed and referenced by new tests (owned by story B)" — it explicitly NAMES its owner (story B = sibling 8937) and is FULLY covered there: 8937 description "Owns the epic's guardrail artifact … commit a documented anti-change-detector guardrail for reconciler tests — a short docs/ note (ADR-style) … New tests reference it," and 8937 AC5 = "A documented anti-change-detector guardrail (ADR-style note …) is committed and referenced by the new tests." Under the G3 ANTI-FP rule (a criterion covered-by-definition by a named consumer counts), this is FULLY covered. Not re-filed.

4-bucket summary for this pairing: FULLY — parent "collapse to single production path", parent "retired path deleted", parent SC "production path exercised, not dead path" (owned by THIS child); CONTRIBUTED — parent "suite green" + parent SC "zero change-detector"; COVERED-BY-NAMED-SIBLING — all schema/contract/round-trip/telemetry/probe criteria (8937/fe57/59ef/2359/822a); STRUCTURAL/AGGREGATE — parent "all six closed; suite green". UNCOVERED — none. No G3 coverage finding for this pairing.
